sources.issuing_organisation: American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons
sources.exact_document_title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Anal Fissures
sources.jurisdiction: United States; international colorectal evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults with acute or chronic anal fissure; paediatric, pregnancy, inflammatory, infectious, malignant, and immunocompromised contexts require separate evidence.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, colorectal, and surgical assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Supports symptom pattern, duration, stool context, bleeding, atypical-location context, and clinician-performed anorectal assessment without diagnosing fissure or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Statement of the problem — presentation and symptom pattern
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.

exact_sections.heading: Atypical fissure context and chronicity findings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-observed location, multiplicity, chronicity features, and context requiring broader assessment without asserting a cause.

limitations: ascrs-anal-fissures-2023: full source unavailable during reconstruction
